title: Yellow Clover
content:

title: To My Country
content:
O dear my Country, beautiful and dear,

    God looketh through Love's eyes, whose vision clear

title: Anniversary Hymn
content:
Our fathers, in the years grown dim, reared slowly, wall by wall
    A holy dwelling-place for Him, that filleth all in all.
    They wrought Hi

title: Above The Battle
content:
Honor and pity for the smitten field,
    The valorous ranks mown down like precious corn,
    Whose want must famish love morn after morn,

title: Matthew Arnold On hearing him read his Poems in Boston
content:
A stranger, schooled to gentle arts,
    He stept before the curious throng;
    His path into our waiting hearts
    Already paved by song.

title: Two Centuries
content:

title: The Pity Of It
content:
I. In South Africa

title: The Falmouth Bell
content:
Never was there lovelier town
    Than our Falmouth by the sea.
    Tender curves of sky look down
    On her grace of knoll and lea.

title: England To America
content:
And what of thee, O Lincoln's Land? What gloom

title: If You Could Come
content:

title: Yellow Warblers
content:

title: America The Beautiful
content:

title: Pigeon Post
content:

content:
